title: Pakmillu
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image= |name=Pakmillu |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]],{{OutboundFlightDate}} ''[[Outbound Flight]]''[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] |species=[[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Judicial Department/Legends|Judicial Department]] **[[Outbound Flight Project]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Mon Cals might look like pushovers, but when Captain Pakmillu says he want something fixed, he means now.|Chas Uliar|}} '''Pakmillu''' was [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the ''[[Outbound Flight]]'', as well as a [[Judicial Forces/Legends|officer of the Judicial Forces]]. In [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]], ''Outbound Flight'' left the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]], and began its journey to the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] for colonization; throughout the expedition, Pakmillu led the massive starship and [[Commander/Legends|led]] the crew and people aboard the vessel. Before the ''Outbound Flight'' reached [[Roxuli/Legends|Roxuli]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Jorus C'baoth]], the leader of the expedition, attempted to kidnap a child in the middle of the night, which prompted the child's [[Parent/Legends|parents]] to form a protest. After [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] discussed the issue, Pakmillu arrived and suggested that they held a meeting the next day about the topic. When they arrived at Roxuli, [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] of the Republic asked Kenobi and Skywalker to leave so that a dispute with Roxuli's government could be resolved by the Jedi Master, which saddened Pakmillu, as he would have liked them to experience the journey. At that point, C'baoth and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] aboard had taken control of the starship, nullifying Pakmillu's authority. Later on, when [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Force Commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]] brought ''Outbound Flight'' out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] with a [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]], Pakmillu was present at the bridge. The Chiss attempted to negotiate with ''Outbound Flight'' so the starship would not pass across the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]], although the Chiss wanted to destroy it so ''Outbound Flight'' did not bring the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] or "Far Outsiders" into [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. C'baoth ignored the Chiss, which forced them to destroy ''Outbound Flight''. Thrawn [[Bombardier/Legends|bombarded]] the ''Outbound Flight'' with his [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|droid starfighters]] and used [[radiation bomb]]s to kill every passenger aboard ''Outbound Flight''. During the bombardment, Pakmillu was slain. ==Biography== ===Podracing=== {{Quote|We have already tried the system, Master Kenobi, […] As Padawan Skywalker said, it works quite well.|Pakmillu to Jedi Master Obi-Wan Kenobi|}} [[File:FateOutboundFlight-TEA.png|thumb|left|250px|Pakmillu led the ''Outbound Flight'' through its journey]] Before ''Outbound Flight''{{'s}} departure, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] visited Pakmillu aboard ''[[Dreadnaught-One]]'', the command center of ''Outbound Flight''. There, Pakmillu showcased the abilities of the command center, sharing the ability to activate or deactivate emergency protocols or systems from there in any of the dreadnaughts without sending anyone to assist the dreadnaught. Kenobi and Pakmillu then engaged in a conversation on the starship's use of a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of [[Swoop/Legends|swoops]] and [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]] instead of [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolifts]], although Kenobi heavily insisted on changing the bikes for turbolifts, Skywalker stated that it worked well, which Pakmillu confirmed, ending the conversation. Shortly after, Kenobi and Pakmillu discussed the starship's future stops, Pakmillu called most of them "flybys," as they would not stop unless there were actual problems. Pakmillu stated the length of their travels, which he estimated to several [[Standard year/Legends|years]], and told Skywalker that the storage core had supplies for ten years, which he expected to supplement along the way. Following the group's discussion on the safety of the possible colonisers, Pakmillu entered a brief conversation on Skywalker getting a new teacher aboard the starship, which was interrupted by C'baoth, which was informed by Kenobi on he and his Padawan joining the expedition. As the Jedi discussed their possible search for [[Vergere]], a Jedi that had become lost, Pakmillu expressed worry on the ''Outbound Flight'' possibly becoming a search and rescue mission. Getting a warning from C'baoth after he expresseed his concerns, Kenobi and C'baoth continued their conversation. Then, the arrogant individual, C'baoth, ordered the captain to arrange quarters for the new members of the expedition. ===Fall of the tyrant=== {{Quote|Captain Pakmillu has arrived. […]''"
"''We'll meet tomorrow after morning meal in Dreadnaught-Two's forward command conference room. […] There you'll both have an opportunity to present your arguments, as well as relevant articles of Republic law.|Jorus C'baoth and Captain Pakmillu|}} [[File:Outboundship.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The ''Outbound Flight'' was crippled during the Chiss attack]] Eventually, before the ''Outbound Flight'' reached [[Roxuli/Legends|Roxuli]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Jorus C'baoth]], the founder and leader of the expedition, attempted to kidnap a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] child in the middle of the night, which made the child's [[Parent/Legends|parents]] to form a protest. After [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] discussed the issue, Pakmillu arrived and suggested that they held a meeting the next day about the topic. When they arrived at Roxuli, [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] of the Republic asked Kenobi and Skywalker to leave so a dispute with Roxuli's government could be fixed by the Jedi Master, which saddened Pakmillu, as he wanted them to experience entering a new galaxy. Kenobi and Skywalker then left the expedition. At that time, C'baoth and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] aboard had taken control of the starship, making Pakmillu's authority non-existing. Afterward, [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Force Commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]] with the [[Core name/Legends|Core name]] "Thrawn" [[Gravity well projector/Legends|brought]] ''Outbound Fligt'' out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] with a gravity well projector, Pakmillu was present at the bridge. Although the Chiss attempted to negotiate with ''Outbound Flight'' so the starship would not pass across the [[Alien/Legends|alien's]] [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|territory]]—although the Chiss wanted to destroy it so ''Outbound Flight'' did not bring the [[Extragalactic/Legends|Extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong|"Far Outsiders"]] into [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]—C'baoth ignored the Chiss, which forced the Chiss to destroy ''Outbound Flight''. Thrawn [[Bombardier/Legends|bombarded]] the ''Outbound Flight'' with his [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|droid starfighters]] and used [[radiation bomb]]s to kill every passenger aboard ''Outbound Flight''. During the bombardment, Pakmillu was slain. ==Personality and traits== Pakmillu was a male member of the Mon Calamari species, he was calm and intelligent. ==Skills and abilities== The Mon Calamari was able to pilot starships, even the likes of ''Outbound Flight'', as well as being able to lead thousands of people in a starship. ==Behind the scenes== Pakmillu appeared in the [[2007]] novel [[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] written by [[Timothy Zahn]]. ==Appearances== *[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] {{Ab|Outbound Flight (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book and [[Outbound Flight (abridged audiobook)| abridged audiobook]]}} *[[Outbound Flight (abridged audiobook)|''Outbound Flight'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Pakmillu |hu=Pakmillu |fi=Pakmillu }} [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Judicial Forces officers]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Mon Calamari]] [[Category:Outbound Flight residents]]
